rewrites.comment: SPA fallback: React Router owns client-side routes, so every path serves index.html.

headers.comment: Security headers for all responses. CSP is tuned for the Vite ES-module worker doing browser-side WASM ZK proving (script-src wasm-unsafe-eval, worker-src blob:) and Firebase Auth popups (frame-src). connect-src is left as 'self' https: until the exact Cloud Run API + auth origins are pinned (Phase 18) — tighten it then. index.html stays uncached so a deploy takes effect immediately (the /assets/** rule below re-grants the immutable long-cache to content-hashed bundles).

headers.value: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval'; worker-src 'self' blob:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; font-src 'self'; connect-src 'self' https:; frame-src 'self' https://*.firebaseapp.com https://accounts.google.com https://*.google.com; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; form-action 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none'; upgrade-insecure-requests

headers.comment: Vite emits content-hashed assets to /assets/**; long-cache them immutably. This Cache-Control overrides the no-cache rule above for hashed assets (last matching header wins).
